title: Automatic connection system and method
abstract: The invention relates to an automatic connection system comprising an electrical vehicle (1) and a charger (2) for charging the electrical vehicle (1) with electrical energy, whereby the charger (2) or the electrical vehicle (1) comprises a funnel (3) extending in a plane and comprising a first connector (5) arranged at the centre of the funnel (3), and the electrical vehicle (1) or the charger (2), respectively, comprises an actuator (11) with a second connector (7) attached thereto, whereby the second connector (7) is orthogonally displaceable in respect to the plane and the actuator (11) is configured for moving the second connector (7) in a linear direction for connecting with the first connector (5).
claims:
Claims Claims 1. Automatic connection system comprising an electrical vehicle (1 ) and a charger (2) for charging the electrical vehicle (1 ) with electrical energy, whereby the charger (2) or the electrical vehicle (1 ) comprises a funnel (3) extending in a single plane and/or in a horizontal plane and comprising a first connector (5) arranged at the centre of the funnel (3), and the electrical vehicle (1 ) or the charger (2), respectively, comprises an actuator (11 ) with a second connector (7) attached thereto, whereby the second connector (7) is orthogonally displaceable in respect to the plane and the actuator (11 ) is configured for moving the second connector (7) only in a linear direction and/or in a linear horizontal direction for connecting with the first connector (5). 2. Automatic connection method for connection an electrical vehicle (1 ) with a charger (2) for charging the electrical vehicle (1 ) with electrical energy, whereby the charger (2) or the electrical vehicle (1 ) comprises a funnel (3) extending in a single plane and/or in a horizontal plane and comprising a first connector (5) arranged at the centre of the funnel (3), and the electrical vehicle (1 ) or the charger (2), respectively, comprises a second connector (7), whereby the second connector (7) is orthogonally displaceable in respect to the plane, comprising the step of moving the second connector (7) only in a linear direction and/or in a linear horizontal direction for connecting with the first connector (5). 3. Automatic connection system according to the previous system claim, whereby the charger (2) comprises the funnel (3) and the first connector (5), which are both arranged on ground (4) and the actuator (11 ) is arranged on the bottom of the electrical vehicle (1 ). 4. Automatic connection system according to any of the previous system claims, whereby the actuator (11 ) is configured for lifting the second connector (7) and/or comprises a spring (14) configured for lifting up the second connector (7). 5. Automatic connection system or method according to any of the previous claims, whereby the charger (2) or the electrical vehicle (1 ), respectively, comprises an additional funnel (3) extending in the same plane and comprises an additional first connector (5) arranged at the centre of the additional funnel (3) and the additional funnel (3) is arranged opposite to the funnel (3) such that the second connector (7) can be either connected to the first connector (5) or to the additional first connector (5). 6. Automatic connection method according to any of the previous method claims, comprising the step of lifting up the second connector (7), and/or comprising a spring (14) configured for lifting up the second connector (7). 7. Automatic connection system or method according to any of the previous claims, whereby the electrical vehicle (1 ) or the charger (2), respectively, comprises a storage shell (13) configured for protecting the unconnected first connector (5) against environmental influences. 8. Automatic connection method according to the previous method claim, comprising the step of moving the second connector (7) in opposite linear direction out of the storage shell (13). 9. Automatic connection method according to any of the two previous method claims, comprising the steps of moving the second connector (7) in opposite linear direction for disconnecting the second connector (7) from the first connector (5), displacing the second connector (7) in orthogonal direction in respect to the plane, and moving the second connector (7) in linear direction into a storage shell (13). 10. Automatic connection method according to any of the three previous method claims, comprising the step of 

 locking the second connector (7) in an orthogonally undisplaceable position in respect to the plane for moving the second connector (7) into and/or out of the storage shell (13). 11. Automatic connection system or method according to any of the previous claims, whereby the funnel (3) comprises, in linear direction towards the first connector (5), a placement area (8) followed by a funnel area (9) for aligning the second connector (7) with the first connector (5). 12. Automatic connection system or method according to the previous claim, whereby the placement area (8) and/or the tunnel area (9) comprise ridges having a fish-bone structure (10) for aligning the second connector (7) with the first connector (5). 13. Automatic connection system or method according to any of the previous claims, whereby the funnel (3) only extends in horizontal direction as single plane, the second connector (7) is vertically displaceable and the actuator (11 ) is configured for linearly moving the second connector (7) only in horizontal direction.
